channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Five takeaways from Trump's address to Congress
item.description: President Trump delivered the first congressional address of his second term on Tuesday evening. It was a chance for the president to underscore the sweeping changes he has wrought in the six weeks since he returned to the White House – and to defend those moves from his many critics.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, The Hill

item.title: China will increase its defense budget 7.2% this year
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — China said Wednesday it will increase its defense budget 7.2% this year, as it continues its campaign to build a larger, more modern military to assert its territorial claims and challenge the U.S. defense lead in Asia.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER BODEEN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Mullin hits 'governor' Trudeau while defending tariffs against Canada
item.description: Sen. Markwayne Mullin (R-Okla.) on Tuesday referred to Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau as “governor,” as the senator defended President Trump’s tariffs against the neighbor to the north.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Polish PM tells European leaders to 'keep calm and carry on' amid US-Ukraine tensions
item.description: Polish Prime Minister Donald Tusk on Tuesday told European leaders to “KEEP CALM AND CARRY ON” amid tensions in the U.S.-Ukraine relationship “To all the European leaders: KEEP CALM AND CARRY ON,” Tusk said in a post on the social platform X.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Pope stable with no new respiratory crises but will sleep with ventilation mask, Vatican says
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis was in stable condition Tuesday and breathing with just the help of supplemental oxygen after respiratory crises a day earlier, but will resume using a ventilation mask at night, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: President Trump’s tariff timeline: What to know
item.description: President Trump’s first few months in office have been filled with sweeping economic policies that overturned the United States’s norms for international trade agreements with countries including China, Mexico and Canada.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Residents flee border area as Afghan and Pakistani forces clash over crossing closure
item.description: PESHAWAR, Pakistan (AP) — Residents fled a border area between Afghanistan and Pakistan after forces from both sides clashed into the early hours of Tuesday, a Pakistani official said.
item.dc:creator: RIAZ KHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trudeau slams Trump for starting a trade war with Canada while appeasing Putin
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau has called U_S_ President Donald Trump’s sweeping tariffs “very dumb.”.
item.dc:creator: Rob Gillies, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Pressured by Trump’s Gaza plan, Arab countries meet in Egypt to discuss alternative proposal
item.description: Arab nations met for a summit in Cairo, Egypt, on Tuesday with the goal of developing a counterproposal to Trump's Gaza plan, aiming to rebuild without displacing residents.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Indigenous migrants in northern Colombia battle worsening droughts and floods
item.description: MAICAO, Colombia (AP) — Standing next to her makeshift home of scrap metal, wood and plastic tarp, 47-year-old Nelly Mengual recounts how severe flooding and winds tore off her roof a few months ago, leaving her knee-deep in water in her home.
item.dc:creator: STEVEN GRATTAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Department of Education clarifies school DEI guidelines, saying Black History Month doesn't violate order
item.description: The Department of Education clarified that celebrating Black History Month is not in violation of an order warning states to remove DEI policies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis to resume mechanical ventilation as his condition remains stable, Vatican says
item.description: Pope Francis will resume "non-invasive mechanical ventilation" Tuesday despite his condition remaining stable, the Vatican announced in an evening update.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UAE Supreme Court upholds sentences issued in mass trial of dissidents widely criticized abroad
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — The United Arab Emirates' Federal Supreme Court rejected appeals Tuesday on behalf of dozens of dissidents convicted in a mass trial that faced widespread international criticism.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Arab leaders endorse Egypt's plan to rebuild Gaza as an alternative to Trump's proposal
item.description: Arab leaders have endorsed Egypt's postwar plan for the Gaza Strip that would allow its roughly 2 million Palestinians to remain in the territory.
item.dc:creator: Samy Magdy, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Timber over Tinder: German forest has connected lovers for over a century
item.description: Known as “Bräutigamseiche” in German, the Bridegroom’s Oak has a famous knothole that's been used as a mailbox since 1892.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL PROBST and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Zelenskyy calls Oval Office spat with Trump 'regrettable,' says he's ready to work for Ukraine peace
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy says last week's Oval Office blowup with U_S_ counterpart Donald Trump was “regrettable,” and he adds, “It is time to make things right.”.
item.dc:creator: Samya Kullab, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Some goods in the crosshairs of Trump's tariffs on Mexico, Canada and China
item.description: President Donald Trump imposed 25% taxes on imports from Canada and Mexico on Tuesday and doubled to 20% his levies on Chinese products.
item.dc:creator: PAUL WISEMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Takeaways from AP's report on Greenlanders resisting Trump's talk of acquiring their homeland
item.description: NUUK, Greenland (AP) — Greenlanders have been pushed into the global spotlight in the weeks since U.S. President Donald Trump said America could take over their Arctic homeland. Most say they don’t want to be American. Many are worried and overwhelmed by the comments and the attention.
item.dc:creator: LUIS ANDRES HENAO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope Francis suffers new breathing crises, is back on noninvasive ventilation, Vatican says
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis suffered two new acute respiratory crises Monday and was put back on noninvasive mechanical ventilation, in another setback to his battle to fight pneumonia, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Costa Rica and Panama coordinate to move southbound migrants
item.description: PEÑAS BLANCAS, Costa Rica (AP) — Costa Rica and Panama are coordinating to expedite southbound migrant transit through their countries along the same route that carried hundreds of thousands north in recent years, officials said Monday.
item.dc:creator: BERNY ARAYA adn JAVIER CÓRDOBA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to expect when China's legislature meets this week
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — China’s annual major political event gets underway this week, bringing together the nation’s top leaders and thousands of provincial leaders to endorse decisions already made by the all-powerful Chinese Communist Party.
item.dc:creator: EMILY WANG FUJIYAMA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump’s row with Zelenskyy recalls Obama, Biden humiliations of Netanyahu
item.description: The clash between President Donald Trump and Ukrainian President Volodmyr Zelenskyy has led analysts to recall the treatment meted out by the Obama and Biden administrations against Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis suffers new breathing crises, is back on noninvasive ventilation: Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis suffered two new episodes of acute respiratory crises Monday and was put back on noninvasive mechanical ventilation, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump again attacks Zelenskyy with potential peace deal in limbo
item.description: There is a growing chorus of Republicans calling for new Ukrainian leadership following Friday's Oval Office meeting.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Delegation of hostages released by Hamas traveling to DC to meet with Trump admin officials
item.description: A group of seven people who were freed from Hamas' captivity are traveling to Washington, D.C., to meet with officials from the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis rested well after overcoming setback from respiratory crisis
item.description: Pope Francis rested well after overcoming a setback in his recovery caused by a respiratory crisis, as he remains in the hospital recovering from pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel is criticized after it bars Gaza aid to pressure Hamas to accept a new ceasefire proposal
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israel faced sharp criticism as it stopped the entry of all food and other supplies into Gaza on Sunday and warned of “additional consequences” for Hamas if a fragile ceasefire isn't extended.
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis off mechanical ventilator, has no fever or signs of infection: Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis is “still resting following a peaceful night" as he continues his recovery from double pneumonia, the Vatican confirmed early Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis stable a day after respiratory crisis, spent 'long periods' off noninvasive ventilator
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis remained in stable condition Saturday a day after a respiratory crisis during his battle with double pneumonia, the Vatican said. He spent long periods of the day off the noninvasive mechanical ventilation he initially needed in a sign that his lung function was improving.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis had peaceful night, is resting after breathing crisis, Vatican says
item.description: Pope Francis, 88, had a peaceful night and is resting after a breathing crisis, the Vatican said Saturday. He has been in the hospital since Feb. 14.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Tate brothers, who face human trafficking charges in Romania, arrive in Florida
item.description: After the brothers arrived in Florida, state Attorney General James Uthmeier said in a social media post that his office would conduct a “preliminary inquiry” into them.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Golden toilet was stolen from English palace in 'audacious raid,' prosecutor says
item.description: It was not your typical smash-and-grab burglary and the booty was precious: a toilet worth more than its weight in gold.
item.dc:creator: Brian Melley, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump says Zelenskyy is coming to the White House to sign US-Ukraine critical minerals deal
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump said Wednesday that Ukrainian leader Volodymyr Zelenskyy will visit the White House on Friday to sign a long-sought minerals deal that will closely tie the two countries together for years to come.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, HANNA ARHIROVA and JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, WGN 9
